messages.content:
What advantage does Virtual Reality provide when compared to conventional meetings using applications such as Microsoft Teams or Zoom?


It affects the human brain in ways more similar to an in-person experience.


It makes it easier for team members to multi-task during meetings.


It is less expensive to implement than conventional Microsoft Teams or Zoom meetings.


It enables more realistic graphics that guarantee meetings are more engaging.
It enables more realistic graphics that guarantee meetings are more engaging.

A client in the manufacturing industry approaches Accenture with an interest in using Extended Reality (XR) for their business.

What is a unique way Accenture could demonstrate our XR capabilities to this client?


Take the client through a virtual space built to reflect the client's specific industry.


Connect the client with an external vendor who can build them a fully customized solution.


Provide the client with state-of-the-art Virtual Reality headsets free of charge.


Show the client a standardized Virtual Reality offering built identically for all industries.
Take the client through a virtual space built to reflect the client's specific industry.

How does Mixed Reality expand on Augmented Reality?


by completely replacing the user's view with a virtual environment


by enabling real-time interaction between real and virtual objects


by running off a headset rather than a mobile phone application


by converting the real-world view into a 2D graphical rendering
by enabling real-time interaction between real and virtual objects

How could Accenture's relationship to Extended Reality be described?


initial caution mixed with gradual experimentation of new possibilities


early exploration that creates an established foothold in new territory


allowing competitors take the lead and adopting their successful strategies


increased collaboration with manufacturers to create new hardware
early exploration that creates an established foothold in new territory

A large retail company hires several new joiners and would like to incorporate Virtual Reality (VR) into their onboarding process.

Which approach would best utilize VR for this purpose?


a 360-degree online tour of the retail store


a simulated experience interacting with customers


an application that enables online contract signing


an animated video that covers compliance training
a simulated experience interacting with customers

What is one reason a fast frame rate is important in Virtual Reality?


to reduce an application's loading time


to allow objects to be manipulated by hand


to reduce the risk of motion sickness


to minimize the time needed to finish a game
to reduce the risk of motion sickness

What is a potential benefit of applying Extended Reality solutions to surgical science?


It allows the visualization of vital information in real time during surgical procedures.


It predicts the amount of healing time needed based on medication and scans.


It estimates the anesthesia required by calculating a patient's body mass index (BMI).


It monitors hygiene by tracking contact between patients and hospital staff.
It allows the visualization of vital information in real time during surgical procedures.

How have Cloud and 5G helped make Extended Reality (XR) technology more accessible to a wide audience?


by using blockchain databases to track and manage user activity within XR environments


by providing a more flexible and affordable way to render quality images at high speeds


by requiring users to upgrade their headsets with new graphic processing units


by making XR applications and devices increasingly reliant on tethered connections
by providing a more flexible and affordable way to render quality images at high speeds

A user wants to visualize a highly complex 3D model using a Virtual Reality headset.

What is the best way for the user to do this?


Use a tethered headset connected to a powerful PC.


Use a cardboard viewer and run the application from a mobile phone.


Use any headset and increase the bandwidth of the Wi-Fi connection.


Use a self-contained headset and lower the headset's frame rate.
Use a tethered headset connected to a powerful PC.

What defines an untethered experience?


the use of self-contained hardware


the reliance on a powerful PC


the need for fast downloading


the use of sensors for perspective
the use of self-contained hardware

A construction-based client would like to develop an application that can analyze an image of machinery and overlay information that will assist in the repair.

Which form of Extended Reality can best assist the client in this situation?


Haptic Reality


Augmented Reality


Mixed Reality


Virtual Reality
Augmented Reality

What does the term "Metaverse" refer to?


a virtual world where people, places, and things interact


the technology used to connect Extended Reality devices to the internet


the central database that stores users' personal information


a model of a Virtual Reality headset with advanced capabilities
a virtual world where people, places, and things interact

What is a characteristic of Augmented Reality?


It requires the user to have a tethered connection to a computer.


It replaces the user's entire field of view with a virtual world.


It overlays digital content on the user's real-world view.


It can only be used with a specialized headset.
It overlays digital content on the user's real-world view.

Why is quantum computing useful for optimization problems?


It uses human intuition to only explore certain potential solutions.


It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.


It evaluates solutions one by one in a sequential manner.


It divides the intractable complexity into bits to calculate simple solutions.
It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.

What is meant by "applied quantum computing?"


using quantum computers to solve real business problems


processing simple mathematical functions more quickly


installing quantum computers directly in company offices


building more advanced quantum computing hardware
using quantum computers to solve real business problems

What is the term for breaking a larger number apart into smaller numbers that can be multiplied together to get a specific result?


interference


entanglement


superposition


factorization
factorization

How is Accenture addressing the emerging market for quantum computing technology?


by taking a "wait and see" approach until quantum computers are developed further


by adopting a Quantum-as-a-Service approach using existing quantum computers


by building our own quantum computers that will be hosted in Accenture offices


by advising our clients to replace all their classical computers with quantum machines
by adopting a Quantum-as-a-Service approach using existing quantum computers

What is a current concern regarding the advancement of quantum computing?


Algorithmic trading may cause stock market instability.


Computers will replace humans in all decision-making tasks.


Existing cryptography may be easily cracked.


Steering qubits towards desired states will introduce bias.
Existing cryptography may be easily cracked.

Why might businesses be interested in using quantum computers?


to run mobile apps more quickly


to solve intractable problems


to create graphics for immersive environments


to process extremely large data sets
to solve intractable problems

Which term refers to the strong information correlation between qubits?


coherence


entanglement


linear combination


quantum annealing
entanglement

Which part of the drug discovery life cycle can quantum computing impact the most?


regulatory approval


clinical trials


laboratory testing


molecular simulation
molecular simulation

How could quantum computing benefit the Financial Services industry?


by predicting market activities months in advance


by determining future value of crypto-currencies


by guaranteeing double returns for fund managers


by enabling complex modeling to optimize portfolios
by enabling complex modeling to optimize portfolios

Why is quantum computing potentially a better fit for weather forecasting than classical computers?


It can function efficiently when stored at high temperatures.


It can store extensive data for better pattern recognition.


It can be easily installed at locations around the globe.


It can perform advanced simulations more efficiently.
It can perform advanced simulations more efficiently.
